roadmap.action: Conduct soil testing to determine nutrient deficiencies and pH. Prepare the land by ploughing, harrowing, and leveling. Apply base fertilizers.
roadmap.resources: Soil testing kit/service. For 2.5 acres (approx. 1 hectare): Approximately 70 kg P2O5, 50 kg K2O, and 30 kg N (e.g., a combination of 150 kg Di-ammonium Phosphate (DAP), 85 kg Muriate of Potash (MOP), and 10-20 kg Urea). Initial light irrigation if soil moisture is insufficient for tillage or seedbed preparation.

roadmap.action: Treat seeds with fungicide to prevent early diseases. Sow seeds at the recommended depth and spacing using a seed drill. Ensure good seed-to-soil contact.
roadmap.resources: High-quality wheat seeds (approx. 100-120 kg for 2.5 acres). Seed treatment fungicide (e.g., Carboxin + Thiram, approx. 300-350 grams for 100-120 kg seeds). Seed drill/planter. Light irrigation immediately after sowing if soil is dry, to aid germination.

roadmap.action: Apply the first top-dressing of Nitrogen. Monitor for early weed emergence and apply pre-emergent herbicide (if not done earlier) or post-emergent herbicide for broadleaf weeds. Conduct the first supplemental irrigation if rainfall is insufficient.
roadmap.resources: For 2.5 acres: Approximately 45 kg N (e.g., 100 kg Urea). Pre-emergent herbicide (e.g., Pendimethalin, approx. 1.2-1.5 liters) or post-emergent herbicide for broadleaf weeds (e.g., 2,4-D amine, approx. 0.7-1.0 liters). Estimated 400,000 - 600,000 liters of water for irrigation.

roadmap.action: Apply the second top-dressing of Nitrogen. Monitor for grassy weeds and apply specific post-emergent herbicide if necessary. Scout for early signs of fungal diseases (e.g., rusts, powdery mildew) and insect pests (e.g., aphids). Conduct the second supplemental irrigation.
roadmap.resources: For 2.5 acres: Approximately 45 kg N (e.g., 100 kg Urea). Post-emergent herbicide for grassy weeds (e.g., Clodinafop-propargyl, approx. 0.3-0.5 liters) if needed. Foliar fungicide (e.g., Propiconazole, approx. 0.8-1.0 liters) if disease pressure is high. Foliar insecticide (e.g., Lambda-cyhalothrin, approx. 0.3-0.5 liters) if pest infestation is significant. Estimated 400,000 - 600,000 liters of water for irrigation.

roadmap.action: This is a critical stage for yield. Monitor closely for fungal diseases (e.g., Fusarium head blight) and insect pests. Apply fungicide if conditions favor disease development. Conduct the third supplemental irrigation if needed.
roadmap.resources: Foliar fungicide (e.g., Azoxystrobin, approx. 0.8-1.0 liters) if disease pressure is high. Foliar insecticide (if needed). Estimated 400,000 - 600,000 liters of water for irrigation.

roadmap.action: Monitor crop health and ensure adequate moisture for optimal grain filling. Avoid any stress (water, nutrient). Conduct a final supplemental irrigation if necessary, especially in dry conditions.
roadmap.resources: Estimated 400,000 - 600,000 liters of water for irrigation (if needed). Total supplemental irrigation for the season for 2.5 acres is estimated to be 1,600,000 - 2,400,000 liters, depending on rainfall and crop needs.

roadmap.action: Monitor grain moisture content. Harvest when grains are physiologically mature and moisture content is optimal (typically 12-14%). Post-harvest field cleaning and preparation for the next crop cycle.
roadmap.resources: Combine harvester or manual labor. Storage facilities. No direct resource input (fertilizers, pesticides, water) for this phase, as previous management ensures a good harvest.
